prompt:
Scheduling asks me weekly why the meeting finder proposed 3am for the Berlin folks the week the US went to summer time and Europe hadn't yet, and I'm done arguing with spreadsheet math. I want a real slot intersector on java.time that computes in instants and only converts at the edges. JDK only, four sibling .java files (no packages): TimeRange.java, Participant.java, Slot.java, MeetingSlots.java.

TimeRange.java — public record TimeRange(LocalTime start, LocalTime end). Fail-fast IllegalArgumentException: "start must not be null", "end must not be null", "start must be before end" (no overnight ranges).

Participant.java — public record Participant(String name, ZoneId zone, TimeRange workday, List<TimeRange> busy). Fail-fast IAE per component: "name must not be null", "zone must not be null", "workday must not be null", "busy must not be null", and "busy must not contain null" for a null element. Store busy as an unmodifiable defensive copy. Busy blocks may arrive unsorted and overlapping — that's normalized later, not rejected.

Slot.java — public record Slot(ZonedDateTime start, ZonedDateTime end) with the same null checks ("start/end must not be null") and a Duration length() = Duration.between(start, end).

MeetingSlots.java — public final class MeetingSlots, one static entry point:
  public static List<Slot> find(List<Participant> people, LocalDate date, Duration minLength, ZoneId displayZone)
Validation, all IAE with exact messages: null or empty people → "people must not be empty"; a null element → "people must not contain null"; null date → "date must not be null"; null, zero or negative minLength → "minLength must be positive"; null displayZone → "displayZone must not be null".

Semantics — this is the part I care about, and the tests pin it against real 2026 tzdata:
- `date` is each participant's OWN calendar date. Their availability is workday.start to workday.end on that date in their zone, resolved with ZonedDateTime.of(date, time, zone) semantics: a time inside a spring-forward gap slides forward by the gap; an ambiguous fall-back time takes the EARLIER offset. Same resolution for busy blocks.
- Subtract each participant's busy blocks from their window (clamp to the window; merge overlaps; a block whose resolved span comes out empty or inverted — swallowed by the gap — is simply ignored).
- Intersect everyone's free intervals as instants; keep maximal common intervals whose real elapsed duration is >= minLength (inclusive); return them as Slots built with ZonedDateTime.ofInstant(instant, displayZone), ascending by start, in an unmodifiable list.
- Never use the system default zone or clock anywhere. All arithmetic in instants — the suite includes days where wall-clock math is off by an hour in both directions (US 2026-03-08 / 2026-11-01, Europe 2026-03-29 / 2026-10-25 boundaries) and windows that cross midnight UTC.

TestMain.java is the acceptance contract with the pinned instants. Hands off. Done = `java TestMain.java` all PASS, exit 0.
